artifact.content_preview:
From: noc-team@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question
Date: March 7, 2026

Hi Alice,

During our review of the Site A migration runbook, our NOC team raised
a question that we wanted to flag to the project.

Currently, the NOC uses SolarWinds NPM for network monitoring across all
GC infrastructure. As part of the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, we
will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into
the SolarW

extracted_tasks.text: Onboard new Catalyst 9500 switches into SolarWinds platform with appropriate polling intervals and alert thresholds.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into the SolarWinds platform

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade SolarWinds NPM module to version 2024.1 to support Catalyst 9500 platform.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: requires upgrading the NPM module to version 2024.1

extracted_tasks.text: Decide if the NPM upgrade should be included in NGIS Phase 3 or handled separately.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Should the NPM upgrade be included as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?

extracted_tasks.text: Review Site A migration runbook regarding NOC team concerns.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: During our review of the Site A migration runbook, our NOC team raised a question

metadata.subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question

alerts.detected_ask: Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'
alerts.suggested_action: The Project Manager should formally decline to absorb the SolarWinds NPM upgrade into NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.35,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'",
  "suggested_action": "The Project Manager should formally decline to absorb the SolarWinds NPM upgrade into NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. A formal scope change request (SCR) must be raised through the SSC Project Change Management Process if stakeholders wish to reconsider. In the interim, the PM should coordinate with the NOC team and the separate monitoring initiative owner to schedule the NPM upgrade in advance of or concurrent with the Catalyst 9500 Go-Live (D-006, April 30, 2026) to close the identified monitoring gap — without absorbing the work into this project's budget or schedule. The risk of a monitoring gap should be logged in the project risk register and assigned to the appropriate initiative owner.",
  "reasoning": "The NOC team's question directly implicates an activity — upgrading the SolarWinds NPM module — that is explicitly called out as out of scope in the baseline ('Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'). The fact that the upgrade is technically necessitated by an in-scope deliverable (Catalyst 9500 switch deployment) does not change its classification; the baseline authors appear to have anticipated exactly this dependency and made a deliberate governance decision to exclude it. Onboarding the new switches into the existing SolarWinds instance (polling intervals, alert thresholds) could arguably be considered in-scope operational configuration, but the NPM version upgrade and associated database schema changes constitute a platform-level change to NOC tooling and fall squarely within the excluded category. Absorbing this work without a formal change request would violate the constraint requiring written Project Sponsor approval for all scope changes and could create untracked budget pressure against the $2,400,000 CAD ceiling. The alignment score is low (0.35) rather than zero because the underlying concern — ensuring the new switches are monitored from day one — is a legitimate and foreseeable operational need connected to in-scope work; the issue is the proposed mechanism (NPM upgrade) rather than the monitoring objective itself."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'
parsed_json.suggested_action: The Project Manager should formally decline to absorb the SolarWinds NPM upgrade into NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. A formal scope change request (SCR) must be raised through the SSC Project Change Management Process if stakeholders wish to reconsider. In the interim, the PM should coordinate with the NOC team and the separate monitoring initiative owner to schedule the NPM upgrade in advance of or concurrent with the Catalyst 9500 Go-Live (D-006, April 30, 2026) to close the identified monitoring gap — without absorbing the work into this project's budget or schedule. The risk of a monitoring gap should be logged in the project risk register and assigned to the appropriate initiative owner.
parsed_json.reasoning: The NOC team's question directly implicates an activity — upgrading the SolarWinds NPM module — that is explicitly called out as out of scope in the baseline ('Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'). The fact that the upgrade is technically necessitated by an in-scope deliverable (Catalyst 9500 switch deployment) does not change its classification; the baseline authors appear to have anticipated exactly this dependency and made a deliberate governance decision to exclude it. Onboarding the new switches into the existing SolarWinds instance (polling intervals, alert thresholds) could arguably be considered in-scope operational configuration, but the NPM version upgrade and associated database schema changes constitute a platform-level change to NOC tooling and fall squarely within the excluded category. Absorbing this work without a formal change request would violate the constraint requiring written Project Sponsor approval for all scope changes and could create untracked budget pressure against the $2,400,000 CAD ceiling. The alignment score is low (0.35) rather than zero because the underlying concern — ensuring the new switches are monitored from day one — is a legitimate and foreseeable operational need connected to in-scope work; the issue is the proposed mechanism (NPM upgrade) rather than the monitoring objective itself.
